_comment: schema: id (string), name (string), x/y (i32 world coords), glyph (single char), color (named: white|red|green|yellow|blue|magenta|cyan|gray|darkgray|lightgreen|lightblue|lightyellow), dialogue (array of strings; advance with enter/space).

name: Old Angler
dialogue: Old Angler scratches his stubble.
"Mornin'. Been fishin' these waters longer than you've been breathing."
"Don't ignore the wells. They're not just for water."

name: Village Kid
dialogue: The kid grins up at you.
"I saw a HUGE fish in the pond past the trees! Honest!"

name: Fishmonger
dialogue: Fishmonger eyes your basket appraisingly.
"Got fish to sell? Bring 'em here."
(Selling not wired up yet - coming soon.)

name: Old Fisher
dialogue: The old fisher doesn't look up from his line.
"Cast far and stand still. The biggest ones are deep."

name: Young Fisher
dialogue: Young fisher gives you a quick nod.
"Caught my first walleye last week off this very plank."

name: The Sailor
dialogue: The Sailor stares past you, at the horizon.
"You need a thousand fish before you'll survive what's down there. Come back when you have them."

_comment: DIMENSION TUTORIAL NPCs. Each gives a quest hinting at how to reach a non-surface dimension.

name: Borin the Prospector
dialogue: Borin taps his pickaxe against his boot.
"There's a way down, walker. The world has cousins beneath it. Look for a big rock with a wooden A-frame nailed to it - that's a mineshaft. Step on the opening and press f."
"The mines have their own people. Don't trust the ones that don't have faces."

name: Dante
dialogue: Dante leans on the well's rim and grins, badly.
"Heaven and earth, walker. The wells go deeper than well water. I've cast into this one - HOW many times now? - and on a hundred casts the bottom opens."
"What's down there is... well. You'll see. Don't bring anything you'd miss."
"Abandon all wait, ye who fish here."

name: Nereus the Old
dialogue: Nereus looks like he's been on this pier since before the pier.
"A thousand fish, walker. Not nine hundred. Not unique - any thousand. Bring that count to the Sailor and he'll take you out."
"Down there the water is the city. The Five Elders preside. Mind your manners."

_comment: VILLAGE TUTORIAL NPCs. Cover the base mechanics without spoiling the bigger plot. Placed in a rough ring around the village square.

name: Reel - Fishing Instructor
dialogue: Reel folds her arms.
"Step on a dock, well, or water tile and press f. A cast bar pops up - the longer you hold space, the further you throw. Release to lock the cast."
"Then wait. A bobber blinks when something bites. Hit space at the bite and the reel minigame begins: keep the green rectangle on the moving fish until the meter fills. Don't let it slip."
"Use a better rod and the fish slows down. Earn fishing xp from every catch, harder fish give more."

name: Stratus - Weather-Watcher
dialogue: Stratus squints at the top-right of your view.
"See the panel up there? Season, time, date, weather. Weather is regional - a meadow gets rain or storms, a desert sees sandstorms, the tundra gets blizzards."
"Some places have weather you wouldn't call weather. Trust me, you'll know it when you see it."
"It changes once per game-day. Plan around it."

name: Skywatcher Vesper
dialogue: Vesper points at the sky for no clear reason.

"Time of day matters. The world brightens and dims as it should. There are two windows when the rarer creatures come up - I won't say which, but pay attention to the top-right panel when it lights up."
"A real game day is four real minutes. Don't watch a clock; just keep fishing."

name: Smith Apprentice Coal
dialogue: Coal wipes soot from his face.
"Yellow D west of here is the rod shop. Buy the next tier with valu - each rod is faster, fish move slower. There are two hundred tiers in wood and metal."
"You must own the previous tier to buy the next. Save up and climb the ladder."

name: Headmistress Lila
dialogue: Lila pats a thick leather-bound book.
"Pink D east is the fishing school. Permanent technique upgrades there - bigger rectangle, faster bites, less escape chance. Real work, real changes."
"The shop sells you a tool. The school changes you."

name: Archivist Quill
dialogue: Quill turns a page without looking up.
"Type :e to open the fishdex. Every species you've caught gets a full entry - where you found it, when, in what weather, what season. Unknown ones stay locked."
"There are more species than you'd think. A lot more."

name: Notary Ink
dialogue: Ink scratches at a tablet.
"Type :t for tasks. Active quests show up; press p on one to pin it - the pinned quest sticks to the top-left of your view as you walk."
"Finish a quest, get valu. Some quests start automatically; some you have to find."

name: Quartermaster Hodge
dialogue: Hodge thumbs through your imaginary pockets.
"Type :i for inventory. Fish go in the Fish tab; plants, minerals, and odd findings in their own tabs. h/l flips tabs."
"Some catches are one-of-a-kind and go in Misc. They'll stay with you forever."

name: Cartographer Mara
dialogue: Mara unrolls a half-blank scroll.
"Type :m for the map. Wherever you've walked is drawn; the rest is fog. Each dimension has its own map - the surface map doesn't show the caves and the caves don't show the surface."
"Walk more. Map fills in."

name: Accountant Penn
dialogue: Penn taps a long column of numbers.
"Type :s for stats. Lifetime catches, escapes, casts, valu earned, steps, every skill level and xp. It's all there."
"Numbers tell you what your hands have been doing while you weren't watching."

name: Coin the Fishmonger
dialogue: Coin eyes your basket appraisingly. (Selling not wired yet - coming soon.)
"When I'm open, walk a fish over and I'll quote you a price. Rare fish, more valu. Unique fish stay yours."
"For now, just catch and hoard."

name: Vim the Scribe
dialogue: Vim grins.
":c for controls. :help for commands. :n for notes. :o for options. :l to leave a non-surface dim."
"Esc returns to Normal mode; type : to start a command. The whole game's a vim window if you squint."

_comment: ATLANTIS - centered around (0, 0) in the Atlantis dim. Five Elders in their throne room; Bluefin Celestial Dragons flank them; guards with golden Y tridents on the walls; citizens around the city.

name: Elder of the First Depth
dialogue: The Elder regards you with a coelacanth's slow attention.
"Walker on legs. You stand in the throne room of the Five. Khei made the water; Fish made us; we made the cities."
"Choose your words. We have been here longer than your speech."

name: Elder of the Second Depth
dialogue: The Elder's eye does not blink.
"The Pantheon split itself into four. We hold its order. You are the test of whether it holds."

name: Elder of the Third Depth
dialogue: The Eldest of the Five.
"You came down to see if we were real. We came up to see if you were."

name: Elder of the Fourth Depth
dialogue: The Elder, watchful.
"You eat us. We forgive only the careful eaters. Are you a careful eater?"

name: Elder of the Fifth Depth
dialogue: The youngest Elder.
"The Fallen Fish writes to us still. We do not answer."

name: Bluefin Celestial Dragon
dialogue: The Celestial Dragon coils, slow and immense.
"Royal blood of Coelacanth. The Five made us their hands and their teeth."

name: Bluefin Celestial Dragon
dialogue: Another dragon. Iridescent and patient.
"We do not patrol. We are."

name: Atlantean Guard
dialogue: The guard's trident gleams gold.
"Walker. Stay within the city. The deep beyond is not yours."

name: Atlantean Guard
dialogue: Another guard, trident raised in salute.
"Pass."

name: Atlantean Guard
dialogue: The guard does not turn.
"Move along."

name: Atlantean Guard
dialogue: The guard's trident points at the sky.
"Move along."

name: Atlantean Citizen
dialogue: An ordinary fish citizen, wrapped in current.
"You came from the dry place. What is it like up there? I cannot imagine."

name: Atlantean Citizen
dialogue: Another citizen, going about its day.
"My grandfather was eaten by your grandfather. We're past it."

_comment: MINES - the crypt. Eerie graveyard, ghouls, ghosts. No castle here, just an old necropolis.

name: Crypt Ghoul
dialogue: The ghoul's jaw works without sound.
"the stone... the stone remembers... the stone remembers..."

name: Crypt Ghost
dialogue: The ghost flickers like a missed footstep.
"I dug down looking for ore. The ore looked back. Do not look back."

name: Crypt Ghost
dialogue: Another ghost.
"They buried us with our picks. The picks rusted. We did not."

name: Crypt Keeper
dialogue: The Keeper stands in the doorway, hands folded.
"All who descend rest here eventually. Welcome."

_comment: INFERNO - The Fallen Fish in his castle, surrounded by infernal subjects.

name: The Fallen Fish
dialogue: The Fallen Fish does not move.
"Grandfather to all standing things. You are mine, in a sense. Sit, walker."
"Fish bound me here. I have made this place mine. The fire is mine. The court is mine."
"You may fish my waters. Take what burns easiest."

name: Infernal Imp
dialogue: The imp grins.
"He fell upward, walker. Upward. You fell down. Funny, isn't it."

name: Infernal Imp
dialogue: Another imp, cackling.
"You will go back up. We will be here when you come back down. We are patient."

name: Infernal Guard
dialogue: The guard's pitchfork glows.
"The court sits. Do not interrupt."

name: Infernal Guard
dialogue: The guard's pitchfork drips.
"Move along."
